steps.html: Open claude.ai/settings/connectors
Click Add custom connector
Name it Speak AI, paste the URL above, click Add
steps.alt: Claude add custom connector dialog with Speak AI filled in

steps.html: Sign in to Speak AI, click Allow on the permission popup
steps.alt: Speak AI connected in Claude with tools listed

steps.html: Open Claude Desktop → Settings → Connectors → Add custom connector
Paste the URL from the top of this page
Click Add, sign in to Speak AI, click Allow on the permission popup

devAlt.summary: Prefer manual setup with an API key?
devAlt.html: Expand Custom headers in the Add Connector dialog and add Authorization = Bearer <your-key>. Generate a key at app.speakai.co/developers/apikeys.

steps.html: Open ChatGPT → Settings → Apps & Connectors → Advanced
Turn on Developer Mode (required while Speak AI isn't yet listed in ChatGPT's app store)
Back on Apps & Connectors, click Create and paste the URL above
steps.alt: ChatGPT connect screen with Speak AI URL pasted

steps.html: For Authentication, choose OAuth, then click Connect
ChatGPT opens a new tab to Speak AI — sign in (or click Confirm if already logged in) to authorize
Return to ChatGPT — Speak AI is now in your connector list
Per-chat: open a chat, click the + / connector menu, enable Speak AI for that chat

items: If the authorization tab lands on the plain Speak AI dashboard with no confirmation message, the authorization still went through — close that tab and return to ChatGPT.
If the Connect button keeps reopening the dashboard, fully close and reopen ChatGPT, then check Settings → Apps & Connectors. Speak AI should already be listed there.
If a chat shows "no actions available," confirm Developer Mode is still on and that Speak AI is enabled in the per-chat connector menu.

steps.html: Click the button — Cursor registers and opens the permission popup
Sign in to Speak AI and click Allow
Click the button — VS Code registers and opens the permission popup
Sign in to Speak AI and click Allow

installs.title: ChatGPT API / Responses
installs.tagline: For developers calling the Responses API directly.

code.lead: Pass your API key in the tool config of your Responses API call:

installs.title: Claude Code
installs.tagline: Recommended — install via the official Claude Code plugin marketplace.

steps.html: Add the official marketplace (one-time): /plugin marketplace add claude-plugins-official
Install the plugin: /plugin install speakai@claude-plugins-official
Activate it: /reload-plugins
Run the getting-started skill and paste your Speak AI API key. Generate one at app.speakai.co/developers/apikeys

devAlt.html:
Skip the plugin and add the connector directly:

claude mcp add speakai --transport http --url https://api.speakai.co/v1/mcp

Claude Code will open an OAuth window for sign-in. To bypass OAuth and pass a Bearer token instead, append --header "Authorization: Bearer $SPEAKAI_KEY".

troubleshoot.summary: Plugin install didn't find Speak AI?
items: Refresh the local marketplace catalog with /plugin marketplace update claude-plugins-official, then retry /plugin install speakai@claude-plugins-official.
If /reload-plugins doesn't surface the plugin, restart Claude Code.

installs.title: OpenClaw
installs.tagline: Use Speak AI as a skill in OpenClaw-compatible agents.

steps.html: Visit the Speak AI skill page on ClawHub
Follow the install instructions for your agent — e.g. clawhub install speakai from the ClawHub CLI
Set your SPEAK_API_KEY environment variable. Generate one at app.speakai.co/developers/apikeys
